title: hackelia hendersonii var. hendersonii
intro: Hackelia hendersonii var. hendersonii, also known as Henderson's stickseed, is a plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae) that is native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. Its health benefits are attributed to its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in herbal preparations, such as salves and ointments, to aid in skin care and wound management.
